title: NEPA Environmental Assessment Authority Rule Pack V0.4
description: Authority-driven v1 rule pack. Every EA review evaluates all 26 Scope=Baseline source records from the uploaded Ingest_Checklist, then evaluates conditional project-specific authorities when package facts trigger them.
jurisdiction: USDA Forest Service Region 1 source-library corpus

rules.title: National Environmental Policy Act applies
rules.question: Does the EA package show that the review is being conducted under NEPA?
rules.requirement: The EA package should show that the agency is conducting environmental review under the National Environmental Policy Act.
rules.package_query: purpose and need for action environmental assessment
rules.source_query: National Environmental Policy Act 42 U.S.C. chapter 55 environmental assessment
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: NEPA section 4332 environmental information is addressed
rules.question: Does the EA package disclose environmental information, alternatives, consultation, or public availability consistent with NEPA section 4332?
rules.requirement: The EA package should disclose environmental information and analysis needed for informed agency decision-making.
rules.package_query: environmental effects alternatives consultation public comments
rules.source_query: 42 U.S.C. 4332 environmental information alternatives consultation public comments
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: NEPA level of review is supported
rules.question: Does the EA package support the chosen level of NEPA review?
rules.requirement: The EA package should support use of an EA by addressing whether significant effects are expected or whether an EIS is required.
rules.package_query: environmental assessment finding of no significant impact significant effects
rules.source_query: 42 U.S.C. 4336 procedure for determination of level of review environmental assessment
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: NEPA timing and unified-review duties are addressed
rules.question: Does the EA package document schedule, review management, page-limit, or unified-review considerations?
rules.requirement: The EA package should document review management, schedule, and unified-review considerations where they are part of the NEPA record.
rules.package_query: schedule page limit unified review objection period public circulated
rules.source_query: 42 U.S.C. 4336a timely and unified Federal reviews environmental assessment
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: 7 CFR part 1b - National Environment Policy Act is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package apply the current USDA NEPA procedures in 7 CFR part 1b?
rules.requirement: The EA package should use 7 CFR part 1b as the controlling USDA NEPA procedural baseline.
rules.package_query: 7 CFR part 1b NEPA procedures environmental assessment
rules.source_query: 7 CFR part 1b - National Environment Policy Act Current USDA/Forest Service NEPA procedures Use as controlling USDA NEPA regulation; check threshold, level, EA, FONSI, efficient review, applicant docs, definitions
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: 7 CFR § 1b.1 - Purpose is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package show NEPA was integrated into decision-making in a coordinated, timely review?
rules.requirement: The EA package should show NEPA review is integrated into agency decision-making and managed consistently and predictably.
rules.package_query: decision-making coordinated predictable timely review
rules.source_query: 7 CFR § 1b.1 - Purpose USDA NEPA implementation Check the EA integrates NEPA into decision-making and uses coordinated, consistent, predictable, timely review practices
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: 7 CFR § 1b.2 - Policy is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package address the USDA NEPA threshold and level-of-review policy factors?
rules.requirement: The EA package should support threshold and level-of-review determinations under current USDA NEPA policy.
rules.package_query: major Federal action discretion significance project at hand
rules.source_query: 7 CFR § 1b.2 - Policy Threshold and level-of-review determinations Check project-at-hand focus, major Federal action, discretion, finality, statutory conflict/function, significance screen, integrated reviews, limitations during review
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: USDA EA requirements are met
rules.question: Does the EA package include the core environmental assessment content required by USDA NEPA regulations?
rules.requirement: The EA package should describe the proposal, alternatives as appropriate, affected environment, and environmental effects.
rules.package_query: purpose need alternatives affected environment environmental effects
rules.source_query: 7 CFR 1b.5 environmental assessments purpose need alternatives environmental effects
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: USDA FONSI support is traceable
rules.question: If the package includes a FONSI or decision notice, is the no-significant-impact determination supported?
rules.requirement: A FONSI or decision notice should be traceable to the EA analysis and mitigation or design features relied on for the finding.
rules.package_query: finding of no significant impact FONSI decision notice mitigation rationale
rules.source_query: 7 CFR 1b.6 finding of no significant impact mitigation
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Efficient and integrated environmental review is documented
rules.question: Does the EA package document integrated review, coordination, or efficient environmental review practices?
rules.requirement: The EA package should document coordination and efficient environmental review practices where relevant to the project record.
rules.package_query: integrated review consultation coordination environmental review
rules.source_query: 7 CFR 1b.9 efficient and effective environmental reviews consultation coordination
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: 7 CFR § 1b.11 - Definitions and acronyms is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package use current USDA NEPA definitions and avoid treating superseded terminology as controlling?
rules.requirement: The EA package should use current definitions and flag superseded terminology when it appears as a mandatory legal standard.
rules.package_query: definitions acronyms terminology NEPA
rules.source_query: 7 CFR § 1b.11 - Definitions and acronyms Terminology used in EA review Normalize definitions and flag legacy CEQ terminology when used as mandatory legal standard
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: USDA final rule: National Environmental Policy Act, 91 FR 17092, Apr. 3, 2026 is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package use the current USDA NEPA rule baseline and avoid superseded Forest Service NEPA procedures?
rules.requirement: The EA package should reflect the current USDA NEPA rule baseline and transition context.
rules.package_query: 7 CFR part 1b effective date USDA final rule NEPA
rules.source_query: USDA final rule: National Environmental Policy Act, 91 FR 17092, Apr. 3, 2026 Current rulemaking context and transition Verify 7 CFR part 1b source, effective date, and removal of older agency-specific NEPA procedures
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Forest Service Environmental Planning / NEPA page is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package align Forest Service NEPA references with the current USDA NEPA rule?
rules.requirement: Forest Service NEPA references should align with the current USDA NEPA rule and not rely on removed 36 CFR part 220 requirements as controlling law.
rules.package_query: Forest Service NEPA USDA NEPA rule 36 CFR part 220
rules.source_query: Forest Service Environmental Planning / NEPA page Forest Service NEPA context and current USDA consolidation Check agency-level references to current USDA NEPA rule and avoid old 36 CFR part 220 reliance
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Seven County Infrastructure Coalition v. Eagle County, Supreme Court opinion is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package apply the current NEPA scope and causation standard for reasonably foreseeable effects?
rules.requirement: Effects analysis should stay within the project-at-hand and reasonably foreseeable effects standard.
rules.package_query: reasonably foreseeable effects project at hand agency discretion NEPA
rules.source_query: Seven County Infrastructure Coalition v. Eagle County, Supreme Court opinion Scope and causation review under NEPA Check project-at-hand focus, reasoned/manageable line, reasonably foreseeable effects, and agency discretion/deference
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Administrative Procedure Act final agency action, 5 U.S.C. § 704 is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package identify the final agency action or decision mechanism for reviewability?
rules.requirement: The EA package should identify the decision, FONSI, or authorization that supplies final agency action where reviewability matters.
rules.package_query: final agency action decision notice FONSI authorization
rules.source_query: Administrative Procedure Act final agency action, 5 U.S.C. § 704 NEPA threshold/finality and reviewability Check whether EA/DN/FONSI or related authorization is final agency action or requires additional finality mechanism
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: NFMA forest-plan consistency is addressed
rules.question: Does the EA package address consistency with the applicable land management plan under NFMA?
rules.requirement: Projects and activities authorized by the Forest Service should be consistent with applicable land management plan direction.
rules.package_query: National Forest Management Act Land Management Plan Forest Plan consistency
rules.source_query: 16 U.S.C. 1604 National Forest Management Act project consistency land management plan
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: 36 CFR part 219 - Planning is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package address the planning-rule framework behind project consistency?
rules.requirement: The EA package should address land-management-plan components and project consistency under the planning rule.
rules.package_query: 36 CFR part 219 planning plan components project consistency
rules.source_query: 36 CFR part 219 - Planning Projects implementing land management plans Check planning rule status, plan components, project consistency, project-specific amendments, SCC where applicable
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: 36 CFR 219.15 project consistency is addressed
rules.question: Does the EA package describe how the project is consistent with applicable plan components?
rules.requirement: The project approval document should describe consistency with applicable plan components under 36 CFR 219.15.
rules.package_query: 36 CFR 219.15 Land Management Plan Forest Plan plan consistency
rules.source_query: 36 CFR 219.15 project and activity consistency with the plan
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: 36 CFR part 218 - Project-level predecisional administrative review is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package document the applicable predecisional administrative-review process?
rules.requirement: The EA package should document notice, comment, objection eligibility, and objection-resolution status where predecisional review applies.
rules.package_query: 36 CFR part 218 objection legal notice decision notice
rules.source_query: 36 CFR part 218 - Project-level predecisional administrative review Projects implementing land management plans documented with a decision notice and FONSI Check legal notice, comment/objection eligibility, objection period, objection resolution, emergency exception if any
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Forest Service National Directives portal is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package use current Forest Service manuals, handbooks, and directives where directives are cited?
rules.requirement: The EA package should verify cited Forest Service directives against current manuals, handbooks, and directive portals.
rules.package_query: Forest Service directives manual handbook current directives
rules.source_query: Forest Service National Directives portal Current manuals/handbooks/directives used by ID team Check cited directives against current national and Region 1 supplements; avoid superseded text
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Forest Service Region 1 field directives index is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package check current Region 1 field directives and supplements where regional direction is cited?
rules.requirement: The EA package should verify cited Region 1 supplements and field directives against current regional sources.
rules.package_query: Region 1 directives regional supplement field directives current directives
rules.source_query: Forest Service Region 1 field directives index Region 1 supplements used in resource analysis Check cited R1 supplements; regional issuance may be more restrictive but not expand/relax national restrictions
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: FSM 2670 - Threatened, Endangered and Sensitive Plants and Animals is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package address Forest Service threatened, endangered, and sensitive species policy where biological resources are present?
rules.requirement: The EA package should document listed, sensitive, or conservation species reviews under current Forest Service species policy where biological resources are present.
rules.package_query: FSM 2670 threatened endangered sensitive species biological evaluation
rules.source_query: FSM 2670 - Threatened, Endangered and Sensitive Plants and Animals Sensitive species and listed species policy Check biological evaluation/assessment, Regional Forester sensitive species, listed species coordination, documentation of determinations
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Multiple-Use Sustained-Yield Act, 16 U.S.C. §§ 528-531 is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package connect the proposed action to multiple-use and sustained-yield purposes?
rules.requirement: The EA package should show that purpose, need, alternatives, and effects are consistent with multiple-use and sustained-yield management.
rules.package_query: multiple use sustained yield outdoor recreation watershed wildlife
rules.source_query: Multiple-Use Sustained-Yield Act, 16 U.S.C. §§ 528-531 National forest multiple-use/sustained-yield decision context Check purpose/need and alternatives are consistent with outdoor recreation, range, timber, watershed, wildlife/fish, wilderness, and plan direction
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Organic Administration Act, 16 U.S.C. § 475 is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package address national forest purposes such as watershed protection and timber/resource management where relevant?
rules.requirement: The EA package should support national forest purposes, including watershed protection and forest-resource management where relevant.
rules.package_query: watershed timber national forest purposes
rules.source_query: Organic Administration Act, 16 U.S.C. § 475 National forest purposes Check watershed protection, timber supply, national forest purposes, and project authority
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: Organic Administration Act, 16 U.S.C. § 551 is reviewed
rules.question: Does the EA package address Forest Service protection, occupancy, and use authority where the decision controls NFS uses?
rules.requirement: The EA package should support use restrictions, special uses, closure/protection measures, or other occupancy/use controls where they are part of the decision.
rules.package_query: occupancy use protection national forests
rules.source_query: Organic Administration Act, 16 U.S.C. § 551 Rules, occupancy/use regulation, protection of national forests Check use restrictions, closure orders, special uses, and protection authority
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span.

rules.title: FLPMA Section 206 land-exchange requirements are addressed
applies_if_package_terms: Federal Land Policy and Management Act
applies_if_package_term_groups: Federal Land Policy and Management Act
rules.question: If a land exchange is proposed under FLPMA, does the EA package address Section 206 exchange requirements?

rules.source_query: 43 U.S.C. 1716 FLPMA Section 206 exchanges of public lands public interest same State equal value cash equalization appraisal

rules.title: Land-exchange statutory authorities are addressed

rules.requirement: The EA package should address the applicable statutory land-exchange authorities, including exchange authority, public-interest basis, acquisition/disposal, reservations, equal-value/cash-equalization, and related federal statutory constraints where a federal land exchange is proposed.
rules.package_query: land exchange exchange of lands FLPMA FLEFA public interest equal value cash equalization reservation acquisition disposal
rules.source_query: 16 U.S.C. 485 exchange of lands in national forests public interest reservations acquisition disposal FLPMA FLEFA congressional report
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span for the land-exchange statutory authority family.

rules.title: 36 CFR part 254 land-exchange requirements are addressed

rules.source_query: 36 CFR part 254 subpart A land exchanges agreement to initiate notice appraisals equal value cash equalization approval title closing

rules.title: Forest Service land-exchange policy and project-reference records are addressed

rules.source_query: FSM 5430 Exchanges Forest Service exchange policy appraisal title evidence reservations outstanding rights exchange agreement closing
rules.evidence_expectation: A supported finding requires one source-library evidence span and one package evidence span for Forest Service exchange policy and project-reference records.

rules.title: Northern Region species of conservation concern page is reviewed

rules.title: 42 U.S.C. § 4336b - Programmatic environmental documents is reviewed

rules.requirement: Check age of programmatic review, reevaluation if older than 5 years, and substantial new circumstances/information

rules.title: 42 U.S.C. § 4336c - Adoption of categorical exclusions is reviewed

does_not_apply_if_package_terms: not subject to a categorical exclusion
categorical exclusion path is not used
